title: The Infinite Simultaneity Chamber

features.name: simultaneity-pool
features.desc-on-touch: You read the surface and see all the versions at once — past reflections, present ripples, futures that haven't occurred yet. The water trembles with the weight of every moment it holds.

features.name: resonance-threshold
features.desc-on-touch: You stand at the place where the echo becomes indistinguishable from the voice. The boundary is warm. Time touches itself here.

features.name: convergence-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: An unreadable plaque. The script seems to write itself in all directions at once, as if every language is speaking the same sentence.

features.name: echo-chamber-wall
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe box transparent and infinite. Inside and outside have become the same volume. You can hear yourself speaking before you speak.

features.name: time-source
features.desc-on-touch: A barely-visible sprite that shifts between what it was and what it will be. You can see both versions overlaid, and neither one is wrong.

inscription: here every echo arrives and departs simultaneously; the source and its shadow learned to occupy each other's breath

file.name: Convergence_AllVersions_1994_Simultaneous.wav
file.note: A recording of a single moment played in all directions at once. The archive cannot determine which version came first.

exits.label: back into deeper dark where echoes become indistinguishable from original sound
downward, where simultaneity resolves into pure sequence
westward, toward the place where language still decides on a single meaning
eastward into the garden where all time-seasons bloom together
